refnamediv.refpurpose: Unit generator for Open vSwitch service ordering

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: systemd-network-ovs-generator is a generator that runs early at boot and when configuration of the system manager is reloaded. It scans the systemd-networkd.service8 configuration directories for systemd.netdev5 files declaring Kind=ovs-bridge, Kind=ovs-port, or Kind=ovs-tunnel, and systemd.network5 files using OVSBridge= or OVSBond= in the [Network] section, including their .d/*.conf drop-ins.

When Open vSwitch configuration is found, the generator emits drop-ins that order systemd-networkd.service8 and systemd-networkd-wait-online.service8 After= the Open vSwitch service, and add a soft Wants= dependency on it, so that the ordering has no effect when Open vSwitch is not installed. The drop-in for systemd-networkd.service additionally pulls in systemd-networkd-ovsdb-acl.path and systemd-networkd-ovsdb-acl.service, which grant systemd-networkd access to the OVSDB socket via an ACL. When no Open vSwitch configuration is present, no drop-ins are generated and the units above remain inert.
The generator does nothing in the initrd.
The name of the Open vSwitch service (or services) the drop-ins order against is set at build time via the openvswitch-service-names meson option, and defaults to openvswitch.service.
